title: Assistance or Distraction? A Cognitive Ergonomics Perspective on Cognitive Load During AI-Assisted Post-Editing
authors: Yao Yao; Tianyi Han; Dechao Li
workflow_stage: AI-assisted post-editing; cognitive load; human-AI collaboration
why_candidate: Directly addresses LLM-assisted post-editing with eye-tracking, key-logging, subjective ratings, and interviews; fills pilot gap on cognitive load and workflow outcomes.
notes: Strong fit: LLM-assisted post-editing, cognitive ergonomics, human-AI collaboration, and workflow measures.

title: Impact of Syntactic Complexity on the Processes and Performance of Large Language Models-leveraged Post-editing

title: Prompting Large Language Models with Human Error Markings for Self-Correcting Machine Translation
authors: Nathaniel Berger; Stefan Riezler; Miriam Exel; Matthias Huck
workflow_stage: human error marking; translation memory; terminology correction
why_candidate: Studies a lightweight human-in-the-loop workflow where a translator marks errors and PE-TM examples prompt an LLM for self-correction.
notes: Strong fit: explicit translator-in-the-loop correction and terminology-focused LLM prompting.
